description_long: This is a roughly triangular, angular stone with a predominantly white surface speckled with fine gray and black mineral grains, resembling a piece of granite or marble. The stone has a jagged, irregular shape with sharp edges and uneven faces, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. One face is marked with the text 'MP-50' in dark ink, suggesting it may have been cataloged or sampled for construction or geological purposes. The surface texture appears coarse and grainy, with visible crystalline inclusions and a matte finish. The stone's size and mass suggest it could serve as a small urban barrier or obstacle, such as those found in parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas. There are no visible signs of significant weathering, cracks, or biological growth, indicating it is relatively fresh and unexposed to prolonged outdoor conditions.
description: A jagged, white stone with gray and black speckling, featuring a rough, crystalline texture and an ink marking on one face. Its angular, natural form suggests use as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its white color with gray speckles and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the stone's thickness and angular profile, revealing more of its jagged edges and crystalline texture.
description_view_2: The face with the 'MP-50' ink marking is visible here, along with the stone's triangular shape and speckled pattern.
description_view_3: This view displays the stone's side, showing its irregular, sloped surface and the continuation of the speckled pattern.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or roadside edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with similar stones as a barrier
attribute_pattern: Fine, irregular speckling of gray and black minerals on a white background, with a hand-written ink marking on one face.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears freshly cut or broken, with minimal signs of weathering or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with paved surfaces or manicured lawns, but may blend with other natural stones in landscaped areas.
